correct_answer: Scientific discoveries and technological innovations have paved the way for sophisticated medical practices.
One key development in modern medicine is the understanding of human anatomy and physiology.
The study of anatomy began to take shape during the Renaissance period.
Andreas Vesalius challenged traditional beliefs and conducted detailed dissections to understand the human body.
The discovery of germs and the development of germ theory by Louis Pasteur and Robert Koch marked a milestone in medicine.
The identification of specific microorganisms as the cause of diseases revolutionized medicine.
The 20th century witnessed rapid advancements in medical technology.
The invention of the X-ray by Wilhelm Roentgen revolutionized medical imaging.
